};
Collections.sort(wordList, comparator);

for (int i = 0; i < wordList.size(); i++) {

private static class WordInfo {
    String word;
    int length;
    int index;

    WordInfo(String word, int index) {
        this.word = word;
        this.length = word.length();
        this.index = index;
    }
}

Then, in the arrangeWords method, I can create a list of WordInfo objects.

So, the code would look like:

public String arrangeWords(String text) {
    if (text == null || text.isEmpty()) {
        return "";
    }
    String[] words = text.split(" ");
    List<WordInfo> wordList = new ArrayList<>();
    for (int i = 0; i < words.length; i++) {
        wordList.add(new WordInfo(words[i], i));
    }
    Collections.sort(wordList, new Comparator<WordInfo>() {
        @Override
        public int compare(WordInfo a, WordInfo b) {
            if (a.length != b.length) {
                return Integer.compare(a.length, b.length);
            } else {
                return Integer.compare(a.index, b.index);
            }
        }
    });
    List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();
    for (int i = 0; i < wordList.size(); i++) {
        WordInfo info = wordList.get(i);
        String word = info.word;
        if (i == 0) {
            if (word.isEmpty()) {
                result.add("");
            } else {
                String first = word.substring(0, 1).toUpperCase();
                String rest = word.substring(1).toLowerCase();
                result.add(first + rest);
            }
        } else {
            result.add(word.toLowerCase());
        }
    }
    return String.join(" ", result);
}

So, the sorting can be done with:

Collections.sort(wordList, (a, b) -> {
    if (a.length != b.length) {
        return a.length - b.length;
    } else {

    }
});

Collections.sort(wordList, (a, b) -> {
    int lenCompare = Integer.compare(a.length, b.length);
    return lenCompare != 0 ? lenCompare : Integer.compare(a.index, b.index);
});

solution:
```java
class Solution {
    private static class WordInfo {
        String word;
        int length;
        int index;

        WordInfo(String word, int index) {
            this.word = word;
            this.length = word.length();
            this.index = index;
        }
    }

    public String arrangeWords(String text) {
        if (text == null || text.isEmpty()) {
            return "";
        }
        String[] words = text.split(" ");
        List<WordInfo> wordList = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < words.length; i++) {
            wordList.add(new WordInfo(words[i], i));
        }
        Collections.sort(wordList, (a, b) -> {
            int lenCompare = Integer.compare(a.length, b.length);
            return lenCompare != 0 ? lenCompare : Integer.compare(a.index, b.index);
        });
        List<String> result = new ArrayList<>();
        for (int i = 0; i < wordList.size(); i++) {
            WordInfo info = wordList.get(i);
            String word = info.word;
            if (i == 0) {
                if (word.isEmpty()) {
                    result.add("");
                } else {
                    String first = word.substring(0, 1).toUpperCase();
                    String rest = word.substring(1).toLowerCase();
                    result.add(first + rest);
                }
            } else {
                result.add(word.toLowerCase());
            }
        }
        return String.join(" ", result);
    }
}
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n log n)

space complexity: O(n)

energy implications: LOW

explanation: The solution efficiently rearranges words by their lengths using a custom sorting approach, ensuring optimal performance and correct casing for the output.
